extraction.one_line_summary: PIDCOCK Thomas attacked 42km out on Monte Santa Maria, soloed for nearly 50km with chasers hesitating behind, winning by significant margin.
tactical_patterns: PIDCOCK Thomas went 42km out on Monte Santa Maria, earlier than optimal but hesitation behind meant chasers never committed fully.
VAN DER POEL Mathieu immediately chased PIDCOCK but was caught back; 2021 winner showed unusual weakness throughout race.
VALTER Attila despite earlier crash looked strongest of chase group on final sector, reduced gap to 7s but teammates hesitated.
Chase group played cat-and-mouse in finale instead of collaborating, allowing 7-second gap to balloon back to 30 seconds.

rider_signals.PIDCOCK Thomas: positive: Bridged to early break on Monte Santa Maria descent alone, then sustained 42km solo effort with high-risk descending
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: negative: 2021 winner notably weak, chased PIDCOCK briefly but immediately caught back, never his usual form
rider_signals.VALTER Attila: positive: Crashed earlier but strongest of chase group on final gravel sector, closed gap to 7s alone
rider_signals.MADOUAS Valentin: positive: Most active chaser attacking repeatedly from group, had legs for sprint finish taking second place
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: negative: Local rider attacked early on Monte Santa Maria with BAGIOLI but crashed badly with 26km remaining
rider_signals.SHEFFIELD Magnus: negative: Crashed alongside BETTIOL with 26km to go, race ended for both contenders
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: positive: In final chase group and sprinted for third place podium
rider_signals.COSTA Rui: positive: Survived to chase group and battled VALTER for fourth place
rider_signals.FORMOLO Davide: positive: Made top ten in ninth after being distanced early with puncture requiring hard chase
